---
title: "SD Card Preparation"
---

# Preparing the SD Card

Before deploying the PolliCam, the Raspberry Pi SD card must be flashed and configured with the core daemon software.

## Flashing the OS

1. Download the [Raspberry Pi Imager](https://www.raspberrypi.com/software/).
2. Select **Raspberry Pi OS Lite (64-bit)**.
3. Choose your target SD card.
4. Click the gear icon to pre-configure:
   - Hostname (e.g., `pollicam-01`)
   - Enable SSH (Use public-key authentication if possible)
   - Set up your Wi-Fi credentials (for your mobile hotspot)
   - Set the locale settings.
5. Write and verify the image.

> **Tip for Field Access:** Because you set a unique Hostname (like `pollicam-01`), you don't need to hunt for its IP address on your phone. You can connect using a stable address based on its ID! Just SSH into `<hostname>.local` (for example, `ssh pi@pollicam-01.local`).


## Software Installation

Once booted, SSH into the Pi using the hostname you configured in the previous step.

```bash
# Connect to the Raspberry Pi
ssh pi@pollicam-01.local
```

Then, simply run this single installation command. It will automatically set up the camera, install all dependencies, optimize the battery, and **convert the camera into a Standalone Wi-Fi Hotspot**.

```bash
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jakubstenc/YOLO_camera/master/install.sh | bash
```

> [!WARNING]
> Because the camera converts itself into a Wi-Fi router at the end of the script, **your SSH connection will immediately drop**. This is expected!

## Connecting in the Field

Once the script finishes, the camera is fully autonomous and broadcasting its own Open Wi-Fi network.

1. On your phone or laptop, connect to the new **`PolliCam-<number>`** Wi-Fi network (e.g., `PolliCam-1`).
2. Open Google Chrome and go exactly to: `https://192.168.4.1:5000`

> **Note on Browser Warnings:** Because the camera generates a self-signed security certificate to allow GPS access, your browser will show a "Your connection is not private" warning. This is expected! Simply click **Advanced** and then **Proceed to 192.168.4.1 (unsafe)**.

### Android "Smart Network" Troubleshooting
If you are using an Android phone, it may detect that the camera's Wi-Fi has no internet and secretly route your web traffic through your 4G/5G mobile data instead, causing the webpage to fail to load.

**To force your Android phone to connect:**
1. Turn **ON** Airplane Mode (to disable the 4G/5G antenna).
2. Turn Wi-Fi back **ON**.
3. Connect to the `PolliCam` network. If a pop-up says "Network has no internet", you MUST tap "Keep connection".
4. Open a completely **NEW Incognito/Private tab** in Chrome and type `https://192.168.4.1:5000`.
